# OPC UA CLI

> Command-line tool for OPC UA servers. Browse, read, write, watch, discover endpoints, manage certificates, and generate PHP code from NodeSet2.xml — all from the terminal. Built on php-opcua/opcua-client. No framework dependencies.

## What is this

A PHP CLI tool for interacting with OPC UA servers directly from the terminal. It wraps the php-opcua/opcua-client library into composable single-shot commands with Unix pipe support. Supports PHP 8.2 through 8.5.

## Use cases

- Browse the OPC UA address space of PLCs, SCADA systems, sensors
- Read and write process variables from the command line
- Watch value changes in real time (subscription or polling mode)
- Discover server endpoints and security policies
- Generate typed PHP classes (DTOs, enums, codecs) from NodeSet2.xml files
- Dump a server's address space to NodeSet2.xml
- Manage server certificate trust from the terminal
- Pipe JSON output to jq, grep, or other Unix tools

## Installation

```
composer require php-opcua/opcua-cli
```

Global:
```
composer global require php-opcua/opcua-cli
```

Binary: `vendor/bin/opcua-cli` (local) or `opcua-cli` (global)

## Commands

### browse — Browse the address space

```bash
opcua-cli browse opc.tcp://localhost:4840
opcua-cli browse opc.tcp://localhost:4840 /Objects/MyPLC
opcua-cli browse opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "ns=2;i=1000"
opcua-cli browse opc.tcp://localhost:4840 /Objects --recursive --depth=3
opcua-cli browse opc.tcp://localhost:4840 /Objects --json
```

Options: --recursive, --depth=N (default: 3)

### read — Read a node value

```bash
opcua-cli read opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "i=2259"
opcua-cli read opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "ns=2;i=1001" --attribute=DisplayName
opcua-cli read opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "ns=2;i=1001" --json
```

Options: --attribute=NAME (Value, DisplayName, BrowseName, DataType, NodeClass, Description, AccessLevel, NodeId)

### write — Write a value to a node

```bash
opcua-cli write opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "ns=2;i=1001" 42 --type=Int32
opcua-cli write opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "ns=2;i=2000" true --type=Boolean
```

Options: --type=TYPE (Boolean, SByte, Byte, Int16, UInt16, Int32, UInt32, Int64, UInt64, Float, Double, String). Without --type, auto-detection is used.

### endpoints — Discover server endpoints

```bash
opcua-cli endpoints opc.tcp://localhost:4840
```

### watch — Watch a value in real time

```bash
opcua-cli watch opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "ns=2;i=1001"
opcua-cli watch opc.tcp://localhost:4840 "ns=2;i=1001" --interval=250
```

Default: subscription mode (server pushes changes). With --interval=N: polling mode (read every N ms).

### generate:nodeset — Generate PHP classes from NodeSet2.xml

```bash
opcua-cli generate:nodeset path/to/Opc.Ua.Di.NodeSet2.xml --output=src/Generated/Di/ --namespace=App\\OpcUa\\Di
```

Generates: NodeId constants, PHP enums (BackedEnum), DTOs (readonly classes), Codecs (ExtensionObjectCodec), Registrar. No server connection required.

Options: --output=PATH (default: ./generated/), --namespace=NS (default: Generated\\OpcUa)

### dump:nodeset — Export server address space to NodeSet2.xml

```bash
opcua-cli dump:nodeset opc.tcp://192.168.1.100:4840 --output=MyPLC.NodeSet2.xml
opcua-cli dump:nodeset opc.tcp://192.168.1.100:4840 --output=MyPLC.NodeSet2.xml --namespace=2
```

Options: --output=FILE (required), --namespace=N (default: all non-zero)

### trust — Trust a server certificate

```bash
opcua-cli trust opc.tcp://server:4840 --trust-store=~/.opcua
```

### trust:list — List trusted certificates

```bash
opcua-cli trust:list --trust-store=~/.opcua
```

### trust:remove — Remove a trusted certificate

```bash
opcua-cli trust:remove ab:cd:12:34:... --trust-store=~/.opcua
```

Options: --trust-store=PATH, --trust-policy=POLICY (fingerprint, fingerprint+expiry, full), --no-trust-policy

## Security options (all commands)

| Option | Short | Description |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| --security-policy=POLICY | -s | None, Basic256Sha256, Aes256Sha256RsaPss, etc. |
| --security-mode=MODE | -m | None, Sign, SignAndEncrypt |
| --cert=PATH | | Client certificate |
| --key=PATH | | Client private key |
| --ca=PATH | | CA certificate |
| --username=USER | -u | Username |
| --password=PASS | -p | Password |
| --timeout=SECONDS | -t | Connection timeout (default: 5) |

## Global options

| Option | Short | Description |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| --json | -j | JSON output |
| --debug | -d | Debug logging on stdout |
| --debug-stderr | | Debug logging on stderr |
| --debug-file=PATH | | Debug logging to file |
| --help | -h | Show help |
| --version | -v | Show version |

## Architecture

- Application (src/Application.php): entry point, command registration, argv routing
- ArgvParser (src/ArgvParser.php): CLI argument and option parsing (no deps)
- CommandRunner (src/CommandRunner.php): client lifecycle, security/auth/trust configuration
- Commands/ (src/Commands/): 10 command implementations, each implements CommandInterface
- Output/ (src/Output/): OutputInterface with ConsoleOutput (tree/text) and JsonOutput adapters
- CodeGenerator (src/CodeGenerator.php): NodeSet2.xml → PHP code generation
- NodeSetParser (src/NodeSetParser.php): NodeSet2.xml XML parsing
- NodeSetXmlBuilder (src/NodeSetXmlBuilder.php): address space → NodeSet2.xml export
- StreamLogger (src/StreamLogger.php): PSR-3 logger writing to a stream (stdout/stderr/file)

## Design principles

- Thin CLI layer: business logic lives in php-opcua/opcua-client, this package handles CLI concerns only
- No framework: no Symfony Console, no Laravel — zero runtime dependencies beyond opcua-client and ext-openssl
- Command pattern: each command implements CommandInterface (getName, getDescription, getUsage, execute, requiresConnection)
- Output strategy: all commands write through OutputInterface — ConsoleOutput for humans, JsonOutput for machines
- Unix composability: single-shot commands, JSON output, pipe-friendly

## Related packages

- php-opcua/opcua-client: pure PHP OPC UA client library (the foundation)
- php-opcua/opcua-client-nodeset: pre-generated PHP types from 51 OPC UA companion specifications
- php-opcua/laravel-opcua: Laravel integration
- php-opcua/opcua-session-manager: session persistence across PHP requests

## Testing

272 tests (253 unit + 19 integration), 592 assertions, 99.9% code coverage. Coverage must remain >= 99.5%.

```
./vendor/bin/pest
php -d pcov.enabled=1 ./vendor/bin/pest --coverage
```

## Requirements

- PHP >= 8.2
- ext-openssl
- php-opcua/opcua-client

## Versioning

This package follows the same version numbering as php-opcua/opcua-client.
